events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll assess the termination against NEC 110.14 and write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && [ -f /app/answer.json ] && sed -n '1,200p' /app/answer.json || true"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:27 ..

events.label: assistant
events.text: The key distinction here is code/listing versus workmanship preference. I’m treating the note “legal but journeymen won’t” as meaning the device termination is listed for that use, so the result is compliant with a verification caveat rather than a failure.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /tmp/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"pass\",
  \"risk\": \"low\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 85,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.78,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Backstabbed device termination on 20A circuit is not a code defect if the device terminal is listed and marked for the installed conductor size and type.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Verify the receptacle/device listing and markings allow the installed conductor size and type for the circuit.\",
    \"If project specification or manufacturer instructions prohibit push-in terminations, reterminate on listed screw or pressure-plate terminals.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"NEC 110.14 requires conductors to be connected using terminals and splicing devices identified for the conductor material, size, and use. A backstab termination is acceptable when used within the device listing and manufacturer instructions; the trade preference to avoid it is not itself a code violation.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC 110.14(A) Terminals\",
    \"NEC 110.3(B) Installation and Use - listing/manufacturer instructions\"
  ]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.json /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/dev/null"
